info.title: authn.sh BAPI
info.description:
The **BAPI** is the server-to-server admin surface. Authenticated
with a workspace secret key (`sk_test_…` / `sk_live_…`), it lives
at `https://api.authn.sh/v1` and handles every operation that
requires elevated privileges — creating/banning users, minting
invitations, managing organizations, configuring webhooks,
rotating signing keys, etc.

The browser-facing surface (FAPI) is documented separately in
[`fapi.yaml`](./fapi.yaml). The two surfaces share the
`components/` directory but have distinct paths, security
schemes, and SDK consumers.

SDKs that consume this surface include `@authn-sh/sdk-php` and
the BAPI half of any future server-side SDK. They generate
request/response types from `dist/bapi.bundled.json`.

tags.name: Email Addresses
tags.description: User-owned email identifiers and their verification state.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Live authenticated sessions across devices (admin view).

tags.name: Org Invitations
tags.description: Pending invitations to join an organization.

tags.name: Org Domains
tags.description: Verified domains attached to an organization.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description: Custom and system roles, scoped per environment.

tags.name: Permissions
tags.description: System permissions catalog (and custom permissions, post-v0.2).

tags.name: Allowlist Identifiers
tags.description: Identifier patterns allowed to sign up under restricted mode.

tags.name: Redirect URLs
tags.description: URLs allowed as post-flow redirect targets.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhook endpoints, deliveries, and the event envelope.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of users in the environment, filtered by
identifier and lifecycle predicates.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending, default) or `-` (descending).
Allowed: `created_at`, `updated_at`, `last_active_at`, `last_sign_in_at`.

parameters.name: email_address
parameters.description: Filter by email address. Repeat the parameter for OR semantics.

parameters.name: phone_number
parameters.description:
Filter by phone number (E.164). v0.1 always returns an empty
list since phone identifiers ship in v0.4.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Filter by username. Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter by `User.id`. Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: external_id
parameters.description: Filter by `User.external_id`. Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text search across email addresses, usernames, first
name, last name. Case-insensitive substring match.

parameters.name: last_active_at_since
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return users active at or after this instant.

parameters.name: created_at_after
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return users created strictly after this instant.

parameters.name: created_at_before
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return users created strictly before this instant.

post.description:
Create a `User`. The BAPI is privileged: identifiers passed in
`email_address[]` are created already verified, and `password`
bypasses the user-flow verification step. At least one of
`email_address`, `phone_number`, `username`, or `external_id`
must be supplied.

get.description:
Return the total number of users matching the same filter set as
`listUsers`. Useful for pagination headers and dashboards.
Fetch a single `User` by ID.

patch.description:
Partial update. Only the fields you send are touched; nullable
fields can be cleared by sending `null`.

rotate_password.summary: Force a password rotation and revoke other sessions

delete.description:
Permanently delete the user, all their identifiers, sessions, and
metadata. Emits `user.deleted`.

post.description:
Permanently block a user from authenticating. Existing sessions are
revoked. Idempotent: banning an already-banned user is a no-op.
Lift a previous ban. Idempotent: unbanning a non-banned user is a no-op.

Temporarily prevent the user from authenticating. Honors the
environment-level `max_lockout_duration` setting.
Lift a temporary lockout. Idempotent.

Upload a new profile image for the user. Replaces the existing
image, if any. Maximum 10 MiB; allowed content types: `image/png`,
`image/jpeg`, `image/webp`, `image/gif`.

200.description: The user with the new `image_url` populated.

413.description: Image larger than the per-environment limit.

delete.description: Remove the profile image and clear `image_url`. Idempotent.

patch.description:
Shallow-merge the supplied metadata buckets into the user. To clear
a key, send it with `null`. Top-level buckets you don't include are
left untouched.

set_plan.summary: Promote the user to the enterprise plan

post.description:
Server-side password check. Returns `200 { verified: true }` on
match, `200 { verified: false }` on mismatch. Subject to the
standard rate-limit bucket; repeated mismatches eventually return
`429`.

Attach a new email address to a user. By default the address is
created unverified and `current_challenge_id` is `null` — the
caller follows up with the challenge sub-resource on FAPI to
verify it. Pass `verified: true` to mint it already verified —
the BAPI is privileged.

self_serve.summary: Add an unverified address (issues a code)

server_verified.summary: Add an already-verified address and make it primary

get.description: Fetch a single `EmailAddress` by ID.

patch.description:
Mark the address verified server-side or promote it to the user's
primary address. Sending an empty body is a no-op.

delete.description:
Permanently remove the address from the user. The owning user must
have at least one other identifier remaining; deleting the only
identifier returns `422`.

get.description:
Paginated list of every session in the environment. The BAPI view
is privileged and unscoped to a particular `Client` — use the
`client_id`, `user_id`, and `status` filters to narrow.

for_user.summary: Every active session for one user

get.description: Fetch one `Session` by ID, irrespective of which `Client` it belongs to.

post.description:
Admin-side revocation. Marks the session `revoked` and immediately
invalidates its tokens. Idempotent.

Admin-side JWT mint. Same shape as the FAPI variant
(`getClientSessionToken`) but BAPI-secured: useful for server-side
rendering pipelines that need to forward a token to a downstream
service on behalf of a user.

post.summary: Mint a session JWT with a custom template (BAPI, v0.7)
post.description:
Admin-side templated token mint. Always returns
`404 jwt_template_not_found` in v0.1; the templating layer ships in
v0.7 alongside the IdP role.

get.description:
Return a paginated list of organizations in the environment,
filterable by member, free-text query, and creation timestamp.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending, default) or `-`
(descending). Allowed: `created_at`, `updated_at`,
`members_count`, `name`.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text substring match against `name` and `slug`.
Case-insensitive.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description:
Filter to organizations the given user is a member of.
Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: created_at_after
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return orgs created strictly after this instant.

parameters.name: created_at_before
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return orgs created strictly before this instant.

post.description:
Create an `Organization`. The BAPI is privileged: pass
`created_by` to record the creator (not required, but
recommended for audit). FAPI consumers use the
`/v1/organizations` FAPI variant which fills `created_by`
automatically from the calling user.

get.description: Fetch a single `Organization` by ID.

patch.description:
Partial update. Only the fields you send are touched; nullable
fields can be cleared by sending `null`.

delete.description:
Permanently delete the organization, all its memberships,
invitations, domains, and metadata. Emits `organization.deleted`.

get.description:
Paginated list of `OrganizationMembership` rows for the
organization. `public_user_data` is populated inline so list
views don't need a follow-up `getUser`.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending, default) or `-`
(descending). Allowed: `created_at`, `updated_at`,
`public_user_data.first_name`, `public_user_data.last_name`.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Filter by role `key`. Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter by `User.id`. Repeat for OR semantics.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Free-text substring match against the member's identifier,
first name, and last name. Case-insensitive.

post.summary: Add a member to an organization
post.description:
Enroll an existing user as a member with the given role. To
enroll a user who hasn't signed up yet, create an
`OrganizationInvitation` instead.

basic.summary: Add an existing user as org:member

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description:
`User.id` of the member. Memberships are addressed by the
member's user ID rather than the membership ID itself, since
a user has at most one membership per organization in v0.2.

patch.description:
Change the role for the user's membership in this organization.
Metadata edits are permitted alongside the role change.

delete.summary: Remove a member from an organization
delete.description:
Drop the user's membership. Idempotent: removing a non-member
returns `404`. Emits `organizationMembership.deleted`.

get.description: Paginated list of `OrganizationInvitation` rows for the organization.

post.description:
Mint an invitation tied to this organization. By default an
email is sent immediately; set `notify: false` to mint a
ticket without sending.

Atomic batch create. If any invitation fails validation the
entire request is rejected; the response describes the
offending row(s). Maximum 500 invitations per call.

Mark the invitation `revoked` and invalidate its ticket.
Idempotent: revoking an already-revoked invitation is a no-op.

get.description: Paginated list of `OrganizationDomain` rows for the organization.

post.summary: Add a domain to an organization
post.description:
Add a new domain. Verification is issued lazily — call
`POST /v1/organizations/{id}/domains/{domain_id}/challenges` to
kick off the proof flow.

get.description: Fetch a single `OrganizationDomain` by ID.

patch.description:
Edit the enrollment policy. Changing the domain `name` itself
is not supported — delete and re-add.

delete.summary: Remove a domain from an organization
delete.description:
Delete the domain. Pending invitations and suggestions
generated by the domain are also cleared. Emits
`organizationDomain.deleted`.

post.summary: Create a verification challenge on an organization domain
post.description:
Issue a verification challenge against the parent domain. Replaces
the old one-shot `POST /v1/organizations/{org}/domains/{id}/verify`
action with the same uniform challenge sub-resource shape used on
sign-in / sign-up / email-address verification — and adds a clean
polling story for the asynchronous DNS lookup.

Legal strategies are `domain_dns_txt` (the server returns the TXT
value the operator must publish in `nonce`, then a background
worker polls DNS until the record appears) and `email_code` (the
server emails a numeric code to `affiliation_email_address`).

Issuing a new challenge supersedes any previous live challenge on
the same domain — the old one rolls to `expired`. Calling this on
an already-verified domain rotates the proof and flips
`verified` back to `false` until the new challenge succeeds.

get.summary: Get a verification challenge on an organization domain
get.description:
Fetch the focused `Challenge` resource. Used to poll the live
status of an asynchronous DNS lookup for `domain_dns_txt`
challenges — the background worker flips `status` to `verified`
once the TXT record is visible, or `expired` once `expire_at`
passes without a successful lookup.

200.description: The current state of the challenge.

post.summary: Answer a verification challenge on an organization domain
post.description:
Submit the operator's answer to a live domain-verification
challenge. The body shape is strategy-specific — see
`ChallengeAnswerRequest`.

For `domain_dns_txt` send `{ }` (empty body) — this hints the
server to attempt the DNS lookup right now. The response reflects
the immediate result: `status: verified` if the TXT record was
visible, `status: pending` if the lookup was queued. The server
keeps polling DNS in the background regardless, so callers can
poll `getOrganizationDomainChallenge` to observe the eventual
flip.

For `email_code` send `{ code: "…" }` with the numeric code the
server delivered to `affiliation_email_address`.

On success the challenge `status` flips to `verified` and the
parent domain flips to `verified: true` with
`current_challenge_id` cleared.

Create a new custom role. System roles (`is_system: true`) are
seeded at bootstrap and cannot be created through this
endpoint — attempts return `422 invalid_field`.

get.description: Fetch a single `Role` by ID.

patch.description:
Edit role metadata. System roles (`is_system: true`) accept
`name` and `description` only; attempts to edit
`permissions[]`, `is_creator_eligible`, or `is_default` on a
system role return `422 invalid_field`. Custom roles accept
the full update set.

delete.description:
Delete a custom role. System roles cannot be deleted (`422
invalid_field`). Memberships referencing the deleted role are
automatically downgraded to the environment's `is_default`
role.

put.description:
Replace the role's `permissions[]` wholesale. The resulting
set is exactly the keys in the request body. System roles
reject this call (`422 invalid_field`).

200.description: The updated role with the new permission set.

get.description:
Paginated list of permissions in the environment. Includes the
thirteen seeded system permissions by default; pass `is_system:
false` to list custom permissions only (custom permissions
arrive in v0.3+).
Paginated list of invitations, filterable by `status` and free-text `query`.

post.description:
Mint a single invitation. By default an email is sent immediately;
set `notify: false` to mint a ticket without sending.

Atomic batch create. If any invitation fails validation the entire
request is rejected; the response describes the offending row(s).

Mark the invitation `revoked` and invalidate its ticket. Idempotent:
revoking an already-revoked invitation is a no-op.

get.description: Paginated list. The allowlist gates sign-ups while the environment is in `restricted` mode.

post.description: Add a literal identifier or a wildcard pattern (`*@example.com`).

delete.description: Idempotent removal of a single allowlist row.

get.description: Paginated list. Blocklist matches always refuse sign-up, even in `public` mode.

post.description: Add a literal identifier or a wildcard pattern.

delete.description: Idempotent removal of a single blocklist row.

get.description: Paginated list of allowed post-flow redirect targets.

post.description:
Add a URL to the allowlist. HTTPS required in production
environments; `http://localhost:*` is allowed in test environments.

get.description: Fetch a single redirect URL by ID.

Returns the composite per-environment settings tree. Secret
credentials (`attack_protection.captcha.secret_key`,
`attack_protection.brute_force.secret`) and any `private_metadata`
are write-only and never appear here.

patch.description:
Update top-level toggles and the writable nested groups. Secret
credentials supplied here are stored but never returned by `GET`.

Convenience endpoint that scopes the patch to the
`restrictions` group. Equivalent to `PATCH /v1/instance` with
`{ "restrictions": { … } }` but doesn't require sending the rest of
the settings tree.

turn_on_allowlist.summary: Switch to allowlist mode and refuse disposable email domains

patch.description:
v0.1 only accepts `enabled: false`. The full schema lands in v0.2
along with the organization API surface.

get.description: Paginated list of every endpoint configured on the environment.

200.description: A page of endpoints. `signing_secret` is omitted on every row.

post.description:
Mint a new endpoint **and** its `signing_secret`. The secret is
returned exactly once in this response — copy it into the customer
side immediately. Subsequent `GET` calls won't include it.

201.description: The new endpoint, with the one-time `signing_secret`.

get.description:
Fetch a single endpoint. `signing_secret` is **never** included in
this response — only the read-safe `signing_secret_prefix`.

patch.description:
Patch any of `url` / `enabled_event_types` / `enabled`. The
`signing_secret` cannot be set this way — use `rotate-secret`.

disable.summary: Pause an endpoint without deleting it

post.summary: Rotate a webhook endpoint's signing secret
post.description:
Mint a fresh `signing_secret` and return it once. The previous
secret stays live for an overlap window (5 minutes by default) so
in-flight deliveries continue to verify; after that, the new
secret is the only valid one.

The dispatcher signs each delivery during the overlap with a
multi-value `svix-signature` header (`v1,<old> v1,<new>`) so
customer verifiers that already accept multiple `v1,…` entries
won't see any failed deliveries.

overlap_seconds.description:
How long both secrets stay valid. `0` cuts over instantly
(use only when you trust the customer endpoint to update
synchronously).

longer_overlap.summary: One-hour overlap during a coordinated rollout

200.description: The endpoint with the new one-time `signing_secret`.

get.description:
Paginated audit log of delivery attempts. Combine the filters below
to investigate why a particular endpoint is misbehaving.

parameters.name: created_at_after
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return deliveries created strictly after this instant.

parameters.name: created_at_before
parameters.description: Unix ms; only return deliveries created strictly before this instant.

failures.summary: Failed deliveries to one endpoint in the last hour

get.description: Fetch one delivery attempt by ID.

post.description:
Re-send the original event payload to the original endpoint. The
replay is a fresh attempt with a new `WebhookDelivery.id` and
`attempt = 1`; it shares the same `event_id` as the original so
customers can correlate.

Common use: a customer endpoint went down for a few hours, missed
a batch of `user.created` events, and the operator wants to backfill
after the endpoint comes back.

bearer_secret_key.description:
Workspace-scoped secret key. Format: `sk_test_<…>` or `sk_live_<…>`.
Resolves to a single `Environment`. Issued and rotated from the
Dashboard or `POST /v1/api_keys` (post-v0.1).

Id.description:
A prefixed ULID. The prefix indicates the resource type
(`user_`, `org_`, `sess_`, `env_`, …); the suffix is a 26-character
Crockford-base32 ULID.

Metadata.description:
Free-form JSON object attached to a resource.

Three flavours exist:

- `public_metadata` — readable everywhere; writable only via BAPI.
- `private_metadata` — readable only via BAPI; never returned to FAPI;
  never logged or embedded in JWT claims unless an explicit
  `JwtTemplate` references it (post-v0.7).
- `unsafe_metadata` — readable everywhere; writable from FAPI by the
  end user themselves.

total_count.description: Total number of rows matching the filter, ignoring `limit`/`offset`.

PaginatedList.description:
Wrapper for paginated list endpoints. Concrete responses use `allOf` to
narrow `data[]` to the specific resource schema.

code.description:
Stable machine-readable code. SDKs match on this string; never
parse `message` or `long_message`.

long_message.description:
Long-form, end-user-friendly message including next-step guidance.
Safe to render in the UI.

meta.description: Per-code structured context (param name, attempt count, retry-after, …).

trace_id.description:
Server-generated request id. Same value goes into the audit log and
into the `X-Authn-Request-Id` response header, so customers can
paste it back to support.

Challenge.description:
A single verification challenge bound to a parent resource — a `SignIn`,
`SignUp`, `EmailAddress`, or `OrganizationDomain`. The parent owns at
most one live challenge at a time (`current_challenge_id`). Challenges
replace the per-parent `prepare-*` / `attempt-*` / `verify` action
pairs with a uniform sub-resource shape: create the challenge, then
answer it (or poll it for out-of-band strategies like `email_link`
or `domain_dns_txt`).

### Parent reference

Exactly one of `sign_in_id`, `sign_up_id`, `email_address_id`,
`organization_domain_id` is non-null per Challenge — the others are
`null`. The non-null pointer identifies the parent resource the
challenge is verifying. The set of legal `strategy` values is
parent-specific:

- `SignIn` — `password`, `email_code`, `email_link`,
  `reset_password_email_code`, `ticket`, `transfer`.
- `SignUp` — `email_code`, `email_link`, `ticket`, `transfer`.
- `EmailAddress` — `email_code`, `email_link`. Used by FAPI
  `/v1/me/email-addresses/{id}/challenges` to verify additional
  addresses on an already-signed-in user.
- `OrganizationDomain` — `domain_dns_txt`, `email_code`. Used by
  BAPI `/v1/organizations/{org}/domains/{id}/challenges` to prove
  operator control of a domain.

### Lifecycle

- `pending` — challenge issued, no successful answer yet.
- `verified` — challenge succeeded; the parent advances its state.
- `transferable` — succeeded against the wrong flow type. The SDK
  should bounce to the opposite flow with `transfer: true`. v0.2
  cases (sign-in / sign-up only):
    1. **Magic-link sign-in for an unknown identifier** — bounce to
       sign-up via `POST /v1/client/sign-ups { transfer: true }`.
    2. **Magic-link sign-up for an existing identifier** — bounce to
       sign-in via `POST /v1/client/sign-ins { transfer: true }`.
- `failed` — too many wrong attempts; the challenge can no longer be
  answered. Issue a fresh one to retry.
- `expired` — `expire_at` is in the past; issue a fresh one.

### Cross-device polling (magic link)

`email_link` is the only sign-in/sign-up strategy that completes
out-of-band. The click can land on a different device than the one
driving the flow, so the originating device polls the focused
challenge resource:

1. `POST /sign-ins/{sid}/challenges { strategy: "email_link", ... }` —
   server emails a click-through URL of shape
   `https://{env_slug}.authn.sh/v1/client/handshake?__authn_ticket=<jwt>&__authn_status=complete`,
   mirrors that URL into `external_verification_redirect_url`, and
   returns a polling token in `nonce`.
2. `POST /sign-ins/{sid}/challenges/{cid}/answer { }` — body has no
   strategy-specific fields; this commits the SDK to polling but does
   not synchronously verify anything.
3. The originating device polls `GET /sign-ins/{sid}/challenges/{cid}`
   (recommended cadence: every 2s for 60s, then 5s for 5min, then stop).
   Each poll returns the same `Challenge` until the link is clicked.
4. The click — possibly on another device — hits `clientHandshake`
   (`GET /v1/client/handshake?__authn_ticket=…`), which decodes the
   ticket and flips the challenge to `verified` (or `transferable` for
   an unknown identifier). The next poll on the originating device
   sees the new status and completes the flow.

Polling does *not* use SSE or WebSockets — plain HTTP polling keeps the
FAPI infrastructure simple and works in every browser. Servers may
rate-limit polling above the recommended cadence (`429`).

### Async DNS verification (`domain_dns_txt`)

`domain_dns_txt` is the strategy for organization-domain proof. The
flow mirrors magic link in shape but is admin-driven:

1. `POST /organizations/{org}/domains/{did}/challenges { strategy: "domain_dns_txt" }`
   mints a Challenge with `nonce` set to the TXT value the operator
   must publish under `_authn-challenge.<domain>`. Status starts
   `pending`.
2. `POST .../challenges/{cid}/answer { }` (empty body) tells the
   server to attempt the DNS lookup right now. Returns the
   Challenge with `status: pending` (the lookup was queued and
   the record was not yet visible) or `status: verified` (the
   lookup succeeded synchronously). The server also keeps polling
   DNS in the background regardless of this hint.
3. `GET .../challenges/{cid}` polling reflects the live status —
   it flips to `verified` once the background DNS job confirms,
   or `expired` once `expire_at` passes without a successful lookup.

sign_in_id.description:
Set when this challenge is bound to a `SignIn`. Mutually exclusive
with the other parent ids — exactly one of `sign_in_id`,
`sign_up_id`, `email_address_id`, `organization_domain_id` is
non-null.

sign_up_id.description:
Set when this challenge is bound to a `SignUp`. Mutually exclusive
with the other parent ids.

email_address_id.description:
Set when this challenge is bound to an `EmailAddress` — i.e. a
signed-in user verifying an additional email. Mutually exclusive
with the other parent ids.

organization_domain_id.description:
Set when this challenge is bound to an `OrganizationDomain` — i.e.
an operator proving control of a domain. Mutually exclusive with
the other parent ids.

step.description:
Server-assigned at issue time based on the parent's state.

- `first` — first authentication factor on a `SignIn` (typical
  password / email_code / oauth / magic-link).
- `second` — second factor for MFA on a `SignIn` (TOTP,
  backup_code, sms_code, …). v0.3+.
- `single` — every other case: sign-up email/phone verification,
  ticket-bound flows, magic-link sign-up, email-address
  verification, and organization-domain verification. There's no
  MFA layered on top.

strategy.description:
How this challenge is being completed. v0.2 surface:

- `password`, `email_code`, `email_link`,
  `reset_password_email_code`, `ticket`, `transfer` — sign-in /
  sign-up.
- `email_code`, `email_link` — email-address verification on a
  signed-in user.
- `domain_dns_txt`, `email_code` — organization-domain
  verification.

Future strategies (TOTP, backup_code, SMS, OAuth, passkey) plug
in here without spec changes.

attempts.description: Number of answers submitted against this challenge.

nonce.description:
Strategy-specific token surfaced to the client. Magic-link
challenges return the polling token clients use to drive
`GET /challenges/{id}`. `domain_dns_txt` challenges return the
TXT value the operator must publish under
`_authn-challenge.<domain>`. Other strategies leave this null.

external_verification_redirect_url.description:
For redirect-based strategies (OAuth, magic-link), the URL the
browser should hand off to. `null` otherwise.

error.description: Last error returned by the strategy, if any.

ChallengeCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /…/challenges` against any parent that owns
challenges (`SignIn`, `SignUp`, `EmailAddress`, `OrganizationDomain`).
Picks a strategy and supplies any fields the strategy needs to
*issue* the challenge (e.g. which identifier to send the code to,
which redirect URL to bounce back to).

The answer to the challenge is submitted separately via
`POST .../challenges/{cid}/answer` with `ChallengeAnswerRequest`.
Strategies that have nothing to issue (e.g. `password`,
`domain_dns_txt`) return a `pending` challenge straight away — for
`domain_dns_txt` the proof token is surfaced in `nonce`; for
`password` `nonce` stays `null` and the caller proceeds straight
to `answer`.

Per-parent legal strategies:

- `SignIn` — `password`, `email_code`, `email_link`,
  `reset_password_email_code`, `ticket`, `transfer`.
- `SignUp` — `email_code`, `email_link`, `ticket`, `transfer`.
- `EmailAddress` — `email_code`, `email_link`.
- `OrganizationDomain` — `domain_dns_txt`, `email_code`.

strategy.description:
v0.2 surface across all parents:
`password`, `email_code`, `email_link`, `reset_password_email_code`,
`ticket`, `transfer`, `domain_dns_txt`. Future strategies (TOTP,
backup_code, SMS, OAuth, passkey) plug in here without spec
changes.

email_address_id.description:
For `email_code` / `email_link` first-factor sign-in, the address to
challenge. Must be one of `SignIn.supported_strategies`'s implied
identifiers. Ignored for sign-up (the address comes from the
attempt itself) and for the `EmailAddress` parent (the address is
the parent itself).

redirect_url.description:
For `email_link`, the URL the magic link bounces back to after
`clientHandshake` consumes the ticket. Must be on the redirect-URL
allowlist. Also used by OAuth strategies (v0.4+).

ChallengeAnswerRequest.description:
Body for `POST /…/challenges/{cid}/answer` against any parent that
owns challenges (`SignIn`, `SignUp`, `EmailAddress`,
`OrganizationDomain`). The challenge already knows its strategy;
the body carries the strategy-specific answer.

- `password` — `{ password: "…" }`.
- `email_code` / `reset_password_email_code` — `{ code: "123456" }`.
- `ticket` — `{ ticket: "…" }`.
- `email_link` — `{ }` (empty body). This commits the SDK to polling
  `GET .../challenges/{cid}`; the verification happens out-of-band when
  the user clicks the magic link. The response is the polled state,
  not a synchronous success.
- `domain_dns_txt` — `{ }` (empty body). Hints the server to attempt
  the DNS lookup right now. The response reflects the immediate
  result — `verified` if the TXT record was visible, `pending` if
  the lookup was queued. The server keeps polling DNS in the
  background regardless.
- `signature` is reserved for v0.5 (passkey).

On a `reset_password_email_code` answer the parent SignIn transitions
to `needs_new_password`; the caller then creates a fresh `password`
challenge and answers it with the new password, which advances the
sign-in to `complete`.

EmailAddress.description:
An email identifier owned by a `User`. A user may have many email
addresses; exactly one is the primary (referenced by
`User.primary_email_address_id`). A new address starts unverified
and becomes a usable sign-in identifier once `verified` flips to
`true` — typically by issuing and answering a `Challenge` against
`/v1/me/email-addresses/{id}/challenges`.

email_address.description: The address itself, lower-cased and trimmed by the server.

verified.description:
`true` once a verification challenge against this address has
reached `status: verified` (or the address was minted verified
via BAPI with `verified: true`).

current_challenge_id.description:
Pointer to the live `Challenge` if one's in flight against this
address. `null` when no challenge has been issued yet, the
previous one was answered to a terminal status, or the address
was created already verified.

linked_to.description:
External / enterprise account links that surfaced this address.
Empty in v0.1; populated as connections land.

reserved.description:
`true` when the address is held by an `AllowlistIdentifier` /
`Invitation` and is not yet attached to a user account.

User.description:
An end-user identity within an `Environment`. Carries the canonical
identifier set (email addresses, phone numbers, external accounts,
passkeys), the auth-state flags (password, MFA), the moderation state
(banned, locked), the lifecycle timestamps, and the three flavours of
metadata.

Phone numbers, external accounts, enterprise accounts, and passkeys
are present in the shape today (as empty arrays in v0.1) so SDKs can
generate stable types up front; the corresponding endpoints land in
later milestones (v0.4 / v0.5 / v0.6).

external_id.description:
Caller-supplied stable identifier. Indexed and unique within the
environment. Useful for migrating users from another system.

image_url.description:
Cached CDN URL for the user's profile image. `null` when
`has_image` is `false`.

has_image.description:
Convenience flag derived from `image_url`. SDKs hide UI affordances
that depend on a real avatar based on this field.

primary_email_address_id.description:
Pointer into `email_addresses[]`. `null` for users created with no
verified email (e.g. username-only).

primary_phone_number_id.description:
Pointer into `phone_numbers[]`. v0.1 always returns `null` (phone
identifiers ship in v0.4).

phone_numbers.description: Reserved for v0.4. Always returned as `[]` in v0.1.

external_accounts.description: Reserved for v0.4 (OAuth connections). Always `[]` in v0.1.

enterprise_accounts.description: Reserved for v0.6 (enterprise SSO). Always `[]` in v0.1.

passkeys.description: Reserved for v0.5 (WebAuthn). Always `[]` in v0.1.

password_enabled.description:
`true` when the user has a stored password digest. Resetting via
"forgot password" flips this to `true` after the new password is set.

two_factor_enabled.description:
`true` when at least one second factor is active. Always `false` in
v0.1 (MFA ships in v0.3).

totp_enabled.description: Always `false` in v0.1 (TOTP ships in v0.3).

backup_code_enabled.description: Always `false` in v0.1 (backup codes ship in v0.3).

mfa_enabled_at.description: When MFA was first enabled. `null` until v0.3.

mfa_disabled_at.description: When MFA was last disabled. `null` until v0.3.

banned.description:
`true` when the user is permanently blocked from authenticating.
Toggle via `POST /v1/users/{id}/ban` and `/unban`.

locked.description:
`true` when the user is temporarily locked out (e.g. brute-force
protection). Toggle via `POST /v1/users/{id}/lock` and `/unlock`.

lockout_expires_at.description:
Unix ms after which a `locked` user is automatically unlocked.
`null` when not locked or the lock is indefinite.

delete_self_enabled.description:
Mirrors the environment-level setting at the time of read; tells
the FAPI whether the user can call `DELETE /v1/me`.

locale.description: BCP-47 tag (e.g. `en-US`). Used to localize emails sent to the user.

UserCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/users`. All fields are optional, but the resulting
user must be addressable: at least one of `email_address`,
`phone_number`, `username`, or `external_id` is required by the
server.

external_id.description: Caller-supplied stable identifier. Must be unique in the environment.

email_address.description:
Initial email addresses for the user. Each is created already
verified (BAPI is privileged).

phone_number.description: Reserved for v0.4. Rejected with HTTP 422 in v0.1.

password.description:
Plaintext password. Server hashes with the configured `password_hasher`
before storing. Mutually exclusive with `password_digest`.

password_digest.description:
Pre-hashed password (for migrations). Format determined by
`password_hasher`. Mutually exclusive with `password`.

password_hasher.description:
Identifies the algorithm that produced `password_digest`. Required
whenever `password_digest` is set.

skip_password_checks.description:
When `true`, accept a password that doesn't satisfy the environment's
strength policy. Useful for migrations.

skip_password_requirement.description:
When `true`, allow the user to be created with no password even if
the environment requires one.

created_at.description:
Override the user's creation timestamp. Only honored when the
caller is migrating users from another system.

primary_email_address_id.description:
Must reference an `email_address_id` already attached to this user
and verified.

primary_phone_number_id.description: Reserved for v0.4. Rejected with HTTP 422 in v0.1.

password.description: Replace the user's password. Mutually exclusive with `password_digest`.

sign_out_of_other_sessions.description:
When `true` and `password` (or `password_digest`) is also provided,
revoke every active session except (optionally) the caller's own.

user_id.description: ID of the `User` that will own this address.

verified.description:
When `true`, the address is created already verified and no
challenge is issued.

primary.description:
When `true` (and the address is verified), make it the user's
primary email address.

verified.description:
Set to `true` to mark the address verified server-side without
issuing a challenge. Idempotent.

primary.description:
When `true` (and the address is verified), make it the owning
user's primary email address.

Session.description:
An authenticated session for one `User` on one `Client` (device).
Lifecycle:

- `pending` — the SignIn flow created the session but the user hasn't
  completed all factors yet.
- `active` — usable.
- `expired` — past `expire_at`.
- `ended` — user signed out on this device (`POST /sessions/{id}/end`).
- `removed` — administratively removed.
- `replaced` — a new session for the same user/device superseded this one.
- `revoked` — admin revoked.
- `abandoned` — never reached `active` and `abandon_at` passed.

last_active_organization_id.description:
Set by `POST /v1/client/sessions/{id}/touch` (or
`setMyActiveOrganization`). Mirrors the `org.id` claim minted
into the session JWT.

organization.description:
Active-organization context for this session. Present whenever
`last_active_organization_id` is non-null — the embedded
`id`/`slug`/`role`/`permissions[]` are exactly what the next
`getSessionToken` will fold into the JWT's `org` claim, so
handlers receiving `session.created` / `session.touched`
events don't need a follow-up `getOrganizationMembership`
round-trip.

**Additive in v0.2** — the field is omitted in v0.1 payloads,
so existing handlers see no change until the producing
environment is on a v0.2 server.

role.description:
`OrganizationMembership.role` for the calling user in
this organization.

permissions.description:
The full permission set granted by `role` at the time
this token was minted. Order is stable (alphabetical).

actor.description:
Impersonation chain. `null` for end-user sessions; populated when a
privileged actor (e.g. dashboard support seat) signs in as another
user. Carries `iss`, `sub`, `sid` of the actor's session.

user.description:
Optional embedded `User` snapshot. Present when the FAPI returns
this Session inside a `Client` envelope so the SDK can hydrate
the active user without a follow-up `GET /v1/me` round-trip.
Omitted on standalone `GET /v1/client/sessions/{id}` reads
(consumers can still fetch the user via the `user_id` field).

SessionActivity.description:
Latest device fingerprint observed on a `Session`. Populated each time
the SDK calls `touch`. Coarse-grained on purpose — no PII beyond the
IP address, which is hashed when stored.

ip_address.description: Caller IP, redacted to /24 (IPv4) or /48 (IPv6) at rest.

Invitation.description:
Application-level (non-organization) invitation. Each invitation
carries a single-use ticket — the recipient clicks `url`, lands on
the Account Portal pre-fill page, and either signs up (transferring
the ticket into a new `User`) or signs in (linking the address to
the existing `User`).

redirect_url.description:
Where the Account Portal returns the user after they accept. Must
be on the redirect-URL allowlist.

url.description:
Click-through URL. Includes the ticket and the
`__authn_status=sign_up` (or `sign_in`) query so the SDK knows
which flow to start. The full v0.1 query convention lives in
PLAN §9.7.

expires_at.description: Unix ms; `null` for invitations that don't expire.

notify.description:
When `true`, send the invitation email immediately. Set to `false`
to mint the ticket without sending email (caller will deliver it
via their own channel).

expires_in_days.description: Days until `expires_at`. `null` for invitations that don't expire.

template_slug.description:
Reference an alternative invitation email template by slug.
Templates ship in v0.5; v0.1 always uses the default template
and ignores this field.

ignore_existing.description:
When `true`, allow creating an invitation for an email address
that's already attached to a `User`. Default `false` returns
`422 invitation_for_existing_user`.

InvitationBulkCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/invitations/bulk`. Atomic across the whole batch:
if any single invitation fails validation, the entire request is
rejected and no rows are inserted.

AllowlistIdentifier.description:
A pattern allowed to sign up while the environment is in
`restricted` mode. The `identifier` is either a literal email
address / phone number or a wildcard pattern (`*@example.com`,
`*@*.example.com`).

When a sign-up identifier matches a row here, the user is allowed
through; when `notify: true`, the row is also used as a
to-be-notified seed for the relevant email template.

identifier.description:
Literal email/phone or a wildcard pattern. Wildcard syntax:
`*@example.com` (any local part), `*@*.example.com` (any
subdomain). Phone numbers are E.164.

notify.description:
Send a "you've been invited" email when this row is added (only
meaningful for literal email addresses).

BlocklistIdentifier.description:
A pattern that is refused at sign-up. Wildcard syntax matches
`AllowlistIdentifier`. When both lists match an identifier, the
blocklist wins.

identifier.description: Literal email/phone or a wildcard pattern.

RedirectUrl.description:
An HTTPS URL allowed as a post-flow redirect target. The Account
Portal and FAPI `redirect_url` parameters are validated against this
list to prevent open-redirect abuse.

url.description:
Origin-and-path URL. Trailing slashes are normalized away. Match
semantics: exact origin + path-prefix; query and fragment are
ignored.

InstanceSettings.description:
Composite read shape returned by `GET /v1/instance`. Covers every
per-environment knob exposed via BAPI today.

Read-only: `private_metadata` and any secret credential field
(notably `captcha.secret_key`, `attack_protection.brute_force.secret`)
are write-only — they're accepted on `PATCH` but **never** returned
in `GET` responses, so they never reach the dashboard or any audit
log unless the caller already supplied them.

enhanced_email_deliverability.description:
When `true`, the instance uses dedicated IPs / DKIM signing for
outbound mail. v0.1 surfaces the flag but always returns `false`.

sign_up_modes.description:
Allowed sign-up modes. `public` is no-allowlist; `restricted`
requires an `AllowlistIdentifier` match; `waitlist` defers
account creation until the operator approves (post-v0.1).

block_email_subaddresses.description:
When `true`, refuse `local+tag@domain` style addresses to
stop a single inbox from creating many accounts.

block_disposable_email_domains.description:
When `true`, refuse addresses on the maintained
disposable-domain list (mailinator, tempmail, etc.).

ignore_dots_for_gmail_addresses.description:
When `true`, `j.doe@gmail.com` and `jdoe@gmail.com` are
treated as the same address for uniqueness.

attack_protection.description: Throttles + WAF-ish toggles. Secret credentials are write-only.

public_key.description: Surfaced to the SDK; safe to read.

require_device_trust.description:
When `true`, sign-in pauses for an out-of-band device
trust check before the session activates.

lifetime_seconds.description: Hard ceiling on a session's age.

multi_session.description: When `false`, signing in on a new device ends every other session for the user.

url_based_session_syncing.description:
When `true`, the SDK wires up cross-tab sync via the URL bar
(used for environments that can't share cookies between
subdomains).

session_token_template.description:
Default JWT template used by `getSessionToken`. v0.1 ignores
this and always issues the built-in template.

test_mode.description:
`enabled` — `*_test_*` identifiers + the magic `424242` code work
end-to-end without sending real emails. `disabled` — test
affordances are off. `rejected` — sign-ins/sign-ups using the
magic identifiers are refused (production hardening).

pre_publish_window_seconds.description:
How long a new key sits in JWKS before becoming the active
signing key. Lets caches warm up.

retire_window_seconds.description: How long a retired key stays in JWKS before being deleted.

organizations.description:
Placeholder for organization-level settings. Always
`enabled: false` in v0.1 — full schema lands in v0.2.

AttributeSetting.description:
Per-attribute policy embedded in `InstanceSettings.attribute_settings`.
`verifications[]` lists the strategies the environment will accept
for this attribute (e.g. `["email_code"]`).

enabled.description: When `false`, the attribute is ignored at sign-up entirely.

required.description: Sign-up cannot complete without this attribute.

verifications.description: Strategies accepted for verifying this attribute.

verify_at_sign_up.description:
When `true`, sign-up cannot complete until this attribute is
verified. When `false`, the attribute can be added unverified
and verified later via `/v1/me/email_addresses/{id}`.

InstanceUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/instance`. Top-level toggles + writable nested
groups. Every field is optional; unspecified groups are left
untouched.

Secret credentials (e.g. `attack_protection.captcha.secret_key`,
`attack_protection.brute_force.secret`) are accepted here but **never**
returned by `GET /v1/instance` (write-only).

attribute_settings.description:
Same shape as `InstanceSettings.attribute_settings`; you can
patch any subset of attributes.

attack_protection.description:
Patch any nested toggle. Setting `captcha.secret_key` rotates the
provider secret; the new value never appears in subsequent `GET`s.

OrganizationSettingsUpdateRequest.description:
Placeholder body for `PATCH /v1/instance/organization_settings`.
v0.1 only accepts `enabled: false`; the full schema lands in v0.2.

enabled.description:
Always validated as `false` in v0.1. Setting it to `true` returns
`422 organizations_not_available`.

WebhookEvent.description:
Envelope wrapping every webhook delivery. Customers receive this as
the JSON body of a `POST` to the configured endpoint, signed via the
scheme below.

## Signature contract

Each delivery carries three headers:

- `svix-id` — message ID (also the `WebhookDelivery.id`).
- `svix-timestamp` — unix-second epoch when the server signed the body.
- `svix-signature` — space-separated list of `v1,<base64>` entries.
  Multiple entries appear during a `rotateWebhookEndpointSecret`
  overlap window; verifiers accept any.

Verifier formula:

```
signed_payload = "{svix-id}.{svix-timestamp}.{raw_request_body}"
candidate      = base64(HMAC-SHA256(decoded(signing_secret), signed_payload))
```

Compare each `v1,<…>` entry constant-time against `candidate`; refuse
the request if no match is found, if `svix-timestamp` is more than
300 seconds away from now, or if any of the three headers are
missing.

## Magic-link emails

Magic-link sign-in / sign-up does **not** introduce its own event
type. The outgoing email is reported through the existing
`email.created` event with the rendered subject + body, exactly the
same as for `email_code` and `reset_password_email_code`.

type.description:
Stable event type identifier. v0.1 shipped the `user.*`,
`session.*`, `email.*`, and `invitation.*` events. v0.2 adds the
organization, role, and permission events listed below.

Future surfaces (OAuth, enterprise SSO, SCIM) will be added
additively under their own milestones.

data.description:
The resource snapshot. Concrete shape depends on `type`:

- `user.*` → `User`
- `session.*` → `Session`
- `email.*` → `EmailAddress`
- `invitation.*` → `Invitation`
- `organization.*` → `Organization`
- `organizationMembership.*` → `OrganizationMembership` (with
  populated `public_user_data`, so handlers don't need a
  follow-up `getUser`)
- `organizationInvitation.*` → `OrganizationInvitation`
- `organizationDomain.*` → `OrganizationDomain`
- `organizationMembershipRequest.*` → `OrganizationMembershipRequest`
- `role.*` → `Role`
- `permission.*` → `Permission` (custom permissions only —
  system permissions are seeded and don't emit lifecycle events)

instance_id.description: ID of the originating `Environment` (so customers can route by tenant).

was_test.description:
`true` when the event originated from a test-mode user / session
(per `Environment.test_mode`). Customers should usually filter
these out in production handlers.

data.name: Export billing data
data.description: Allow exporting invoices and usage reports.

WebhookEndpoint.description:
A customer-controlled HTTPS endpoint the dispatcher delivers events
to. Each endpoint has its own `signing_secret` so customers can rotate
one without disturbing others.

`signing_secret` is **write-only**: it appears once on `createWebhookEndpoint`
and once on `rotateWebhookEndpointSecret`, never on subsequent
`getWebhookEndpoint` / `listWebhookEndpoints` reads.

url.description: HTTPS URL the dispatcher POSTs events to.

signing_secret.description:
Per-endpoint HMAC-SHA256 secret. Format: `whsec_<base64>`.
Returned exactly once, on create and on rotate. Subsequent reads
omit this field entirely.

signing_secret_prefix.description:
Read-safe partial fingerprint (first 8 chars of `signing_secret`)
so the dashboard can display "ends in `…dGVz`" without exposing
the secret.

enabled_event_types.description:
Subset of the `WebhookEvent.type` enum the endpoint subscribes
to. `["*"]` means "every event in every milestone" and is the
simplest way to opt into v0.2 organization events without
revisiting subscriptions on every release.

Wildcards at the resource level are also accepted —
`"organization.*"` matches every `organization.*` event,
`"organizationMembership.*"` matches the membership lifecycle,
etc. Wildcard expansion is evaluated at delivery time so an
endpoint listed with `organization.*` automatically receives
future event types added to the resource without an explicit
update.

enabled.description:
When `false`, the dispatcher skips this endpoint without retrying.
Toggled from the dashboard during incidents.

rotation_window_expires_at.description:
When set, the prior `signing_secret` is still accepted on
replay until this timestamp. Cleared once the rotation
window closes.

disabled_at.description:
Set by the dispatcher when an endpoint is auto-disabled
after sustained delivery failures. `null` while healthy.

url.description:
HTTPS URL. The dispatcher refuses non-HTTPS targets in production
environments; `http://localhost:*` is allowed in test environments.

enabled_event_types.description:
Initial subscription set. Use `["*"]` to subscribe to every event
in the v0.1 catalog.

WebhookDelivery.description:
One attempt at delivering one event to one endpoint. The dispatcher
retries with exponential backoff on transient failures; each retry
produces a new `WebhookDelivery` row sharing the `event_id` of the
first attempt.

event_id.description: Stable across all retry attempts of the same event.

attempt.description: 1-based attempt counter; increments on every retry.

response_status.description: HTTP status returned by the endpoint, or `null` for connection failures.

response_body.description:
First 4 KiB of the endpoint's response body (UTF-8 best-effort).
Stored for debugging — never matched against in dispatcher logic.

next_retry_at.description: Unix ms; `null` when delivery succeeded or was given up on.

succeeded.description:
`true` once the endpoint returns 2xx. Permanent failures
(`succeeded: false` and `next_retry_at: null`) are kept for
audit but no longer retried.

PublicUserData.description:
Minimal user summary embedded in organization-related resources
so list views don't need a follow-up `GET /v1/users/{id}`. All
fields mirror the corresponding `User` fields at the time of
read.

identifier.description:
Best-available human-readable handle for the user — the
primary email address, or username if no email is set.

user_id.description:
`User.id`. Always present for memberships; on
`OrganizationMembershipRequest.public_user_data` it's set as
soon as the requester signs up.

Organization.description:
An organization within an `Environment`. Groups users via
`OrganizationMembership` and carries its own roles, invitations,
and verified domains.

The slug is unique per environment and is what end users see in
routing-aware components (e.g. `<OrganizationProfile />` deep
links). `members_count` and `pending_invitations_count` are
denormalized so list views don't need a follow-up aggregation.

slug.description:
Unique within the environment. Used in Account Portal URLs
(`/organization/<slug>`) and as the routing key for
`<OrganizationProfile />`.

image_url.description:
Cached CDN URL for the organization's logo. `null` when
`has_image` is `false`.

pending_invitations_count.description:
`OrganizationInvitation` rows still in `pending` status. Does
not include accepted, revoked, or expired invitations.

max_allowed_memberships.description:
Cap on `members_count`. `null` (default) means no cap. `0`
effectively freezes the org. Reaching the cap returns
`422 organization_membership_quota_exceeded` on further
`POST /memberships` and `POST /invitations`.

admin_delete_enabled.description:
Mirrors the environment-level setting at the time of read;
tells the dashboard whether `org:admin` members can
`DELETE /v1/organizations/{id}` themselves (as opposed to
requiring a workspace operator).

created_by.description:
`User.id` of the member who created the organization. `null`
when created via BAPI without an `actor` user.

OrganizationCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/organizations` (BAPI) and
`POST /v1/organizations` (FAPI).

On BAPI, `created_by` is required. On FAPI, the server fills it
with the calling user automatically; passing it is rejected.

slug.description:
Optional. When omitted, the server slugifies `name` and
appends a short suffix to disambiguate.

created_by.description:
`User.id` to record as the creator. **BAPI only.** FAPI
requests that include this field are rejected with
`422 invalid_field`.

OrganizationUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/organizations/{id}` (BAPI) and the FAPI
equivalent. All fields are optional; omitted fields are
unchanged.

admin_delete_enabled.description: BAPI only — FAPI updates that include this field are rejected.

OrganizationMembership.description:
Join row tying a `User` to an `Organization`. Carries a single
`role` (the role's `key`) plus the *computed* `permissions[]`
derived from that role at read time — consumers should not cache
the permissions list across role changes.

v0.2 supports exactly one role per membership; multi-role per
membership is post-v1.0.

role.description:
The role's stable `key` (e.g. `org:admin`, `org:member`, or
a custom `org:billing`). Mutating this field requires the
caller to hold `org:sys_memberships:manage` in the same
organization.

role_name.description: Human-readable display name for the role at the time of read.

permissions.description:
The full permission set granted to this member, computed by
walking the role's permissions at read time. Order is stable
(alphabetical) so SDK comparisons are deterministic.

OrganizationMembershipCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/organizations/{org_id}/memberships`. Adds an
existing user to the organization as a member with the given
role.

To enroll a user who hasn't signed up yet, create an
`OrganizationInvitation` instead.

OrganizationMembershipUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/organizations/{org_id}/memberships/{id}`. v0.2
supports changing the role; metadata edits are fire-and-forget.

OrganizationInvitation.description:
Pending invitation for a future member to join an
`Organization`. The recipient clicks `url`, lands on the Account
Portal pre-fill page, and either signs up (transferring the
ticket into a new `User` and creating the membership) or signs
in (creating the membership against the existing `User`).

Mirrors the application-level `Invitation` ticket convention
from PLAN §9.7 — `__authn_status=sign_up` is appended for
recipients who don't yet have a `User`.

role.description: Role `key` the recipient receives on accept.

role_name.description: Human-readable display name for `role` at the time of read.

inviter_user_id.description:
`User.id` of the org member who issued the invitation. `null`
for invitations created via BAPI without an `actor` user.

redirect_url.description:
Where the Account Portal returns the recipient after they
accept. Must match the redirect-URL allowlist.

url.description:
Click-through URL. Carries the ticket plus
`__authn_status=sign_up` (or `sign_in` when the email is
already attached to a `User`) and the org id, so the SDK
knows which flow to start.

Returned for `pending` invitations only. `null` once the
invitation is `revoked` / `accepted` / `expired` since the
ticket can no longer be exchanged.

expires_at.description: Unix ms; `null` for invitations that don't expire.

role.description: Role `key` the recipient receives on accept.

inviter_user_id.description:
Override the recorded inviter. **BAPI only** — FAPI pulls
this from the calling session and rejects requests that set
it explicitly.

notify.description:
When `true`, send the invitation email immediately. Set to
`false` to mint the ticket without sending email.

expires_in_days.description: Days until `expires_at`. `null` for invitations that don't expire.

OrganizationInvitationBulkCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/organizations/{org_id}/invitations/bulk`.
Atomic across the whole batch: if any single invitation fails
validation, the entire request is rejected and no rows are
inserted.

OrganizationDomain.description:
A domain attached to an `Organization`. Once verified, drives
domain-based enrollment — depending on `enrollment_mode`, sign-ups
with a matching email get an automatic invitation, an automatic
membership-request suggestion, or nothing at all.

Verification runs through a `Challenge` against
`/v1/organizations/{org}/domains/{id}/challenges`. The proof channel
(DNS TXT or email-to-`affiliation_email_address`) is selected by the
challenge's `strategy` (`domain_dns_txt` or `email_code`).

name.description:
The domain (no scheme, no path) — e.g. `acme.com`. Lowercased
and IDN-normalized on write.

verified.description:
`true` once a verification challenge against this domain has
reached `status: verified`.

current_challenge_id.description:
Pointer to the live `Challenge` if one's in flight against this
domain. `null` when no challenge has been issued yet, the
previous one was answered to a terminal status, or the operator
has rotated the proof token.

enrollment_mode.description:
- `manual_invitation` — domain match has no effect on sign-up;
  members must be invited explicitly.
- `automatic_invitation` — sign-ups with a matching email
  skip the invitation step and join as `default_role`.
- `automatic_suggestion` — sign-ups with a matching email get
  an `OrganizationMembershipRequest` row that an admin must
  approve.

affiliation_email_address.description:
Address used as the `email_code` verification target when the
domain is being verified via the `email_code` challenge
strategy. Always sits at the domain being verified
(e.g. `admin@acme.com`).

total_pending_invitations.description:
Pending `OrganizationInvitation` rows generated by this
domain when `enrollment_mode === 'automatic_invitation'`.

total_pending_suggestions.description:
Pending `OrganizationMembershipRequest` rows generated by
this domain when `enrollment_mode === 'automatic_suggestion'`.

affiliation_email_address.description:
Optional address to use as the `email_code` verification target
when an operator later issues an `email_code` challenge against
this domain. Must sit at the domain being verified.

OrganizationDomainUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/organizations/{org_id}/domains/{id}`. Only the
enrollment policy is editable post-create; to change the domain
name itself, delete and re-add.

OrganizationMembershipRequest.description:
Pending request from a user with a verified domain match to join
an `Organization`. Created automatically when a user signs up
(or adds a verified email) whose address matches an
`OrganizationDomain` whose `enrollment_mode` is
`automatic_suggestion`.

An admin with `org:sys_memberships:manage` accepts the request
to convert it into an `OrganizationMembership`.

Permission.description:
An atomic capability. Members hold permissions transitively
through their `OrganizationMembership.role`. The
`org:<feature>:<verb>` naming is a convention, not enforced by
schema, so dashboards can list custom permissions cleanly.

System permissions (`is_system: true`) are seeded per environment
at bootstrap and cannot be edited or deleted. Custom permissions
are reserved for v0.3+ — v0.2 ships only the system catalog.

### System permissions catalog (v0.2)

Every environment is seeded with exactly these thirteen system
permissions:

| `key` | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `org:sys_profile:read` | Read org name, slug, image, metadata. |
| `org:sys_profile:manage` | Edit org name, slug, image, metadata. |
| `org:sys_profile:delete` | Delete the org (subject to `admin_delete_enabled`). |
| `org:sys_memberships:read` | List members and pending requests. |
| `org:sys_memberships:manage` | Invite, remove, change roles, accept membership requests. |
| `org:sys_invitations:read` | List outgoing invitations. |
| `org:sys_invitations:manage` | Create, revoke, and resend invitations. |
| `org:sys_domains:read` | List verified domains. |
| `org:sys_domains:manage` | Add, verify, change enrollment mode, delete domains. |
| `org:sys_roles:read` | List roles and their permission assignments. |
| `org:sys_roles:manage` | Create, update, and delete roles; assign permissions. |
| `org:sys_permissions:read` | List available permissions. |
| `org:sys_permissions:manage` | Create and delete custom permissions. |

key.description:
Stable, lowercase, snake-case identifier in the form
`org:<feature>:<verb>`. System permissions live under the
reserved `org:sys_*` prefix.

is_system.description:
`true` for the seeded system catalog above. System
permissions cannot be edited or deleted; only their `name`
and `description` may be localized at render time.

Role.description:
A bundle of permissions assignable to an `OrganizationMembership`.
Each environment is seeded with two default roles at bootstrap
(`org:admin` and `org:member`); custom roles are scoped to the
environment and shared across all its organizations.

### Default roles (v0.2)

| `key` | Permissions | `is_creator_eligible` | `is_default` |
|---|---|:-:|:-:|
| `org:admin` | All thirteen `org:sys_*` system permissions. | `true` | `false` |
| `org:member` | `org:sys_profile:read`, `org:sys_memberships:read`, `org:sys_domains:read` *(read-only subset; v0.2 introduces the implicit `:read` companion to each `sys_*:manage` system permission as a separate seeded row)*. | `false` | `true` |

`is_creator_eligible` controls which roles can be picked as the
org's `creator_role`; `is_default` flags the role assigned to
members enrolled via `automatic_invitation` or accepted
`OrganizationMembershipRequest`.

key.description:
Stable, lowercase, snake-case identifier in the form
`org:<name>`. Defaults: `org:admin`, `org:member`.

is_creator_eligible.description:
Whether this role may be picked as
`Organization.creator_role`. The seeded `org:admin` role is
eligible; `org:member` is not.

is_default.description:
Whether this role is the implicit role for members enrolled
automatically (`automatic_invitation`,
`OrganizationMembershipRequest` accept). Exactly one role per
environment carries `is_default: true`.

is_system.description:
`true` for the seeded `org:admin` and `org:member` rows.
System roles' `permissions[]` cannot be edited; only `name`
and `description` may be customized.

permissions.description:
The role's permission set as a list of `Permission.key`
strings (not full `Permission` objects). Order is stable
(alphabetical) for deterministic comparisons.

RoleCreateRequest.description:
Body for `POST /v1/roles` (BAPI). Custom roles only — system
roles are seeded and not creatable through the API.

key.description:
Custom keys must not collide with the reserved
`org:admin` / `org:member` defaults or anything under the
`org:sys_*` permission prefix.

is_default.description:
Setting this to `true` clears the flag on whichever role
currently holds it (exactly one default per environment).

permissions.description:
The role's permission set as `Permission.key` strings. Each
key must already exist (system or custom) at the time of
write.

RoleUpdateRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /v1/roles/{id}` (BAPI). System roles allow only
`name` / `description` edits; custom roles allow the full set.

RolePermissionsRequest.description:
Body for `PUT /v1/roles/{role_id}/permissions`. Replaces the
role's permission set wholesale — the resulting set is exactly
the keys you send. Pass `[]` to clear all permissions.

permissions.description:
Permission `key` strings. Each must already exist (system or
custom) at the time of write; missing keys return
`422 invalid_field` and the role is not modified.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not authorized for the action.

NotFound.description: Resource does not exist (or is not visible to the caller).

Conflict.description: State conflict — e.g. an `Idempotency-Key` reuse with a different request body.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the next token in the bucket.

LimitParam.name: limit
LimitParam.description: Maximum rows to return. Default `10`, maximum `500`.

OffsetParam.name: offset
OffsetParam.description: Rows to skip before returning results.

OrderByParam.name: order_by
OrderByParam.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending) or `-` (descending). Per-resource
endpoints document the allowed keys.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description:
Caller-supplied UUID for `POST` mutations. The server caches the response
for 24 hours; replays with the same key return the cached response without
re-executing. A different request body under the same key returns `409`.
